**Monday (Industry Insight)** — Water infrastructure spending in WA/AU. Angle: m draft

Every bore starts with the same question: what's the ground going to throw at you?
Across WA and Queensland, more infrastructure is going underground. Power. Water. Comms. That means more HDD crews running longer bores through ground they haven't seen before.

The crews winning work right now aren't just good drillers. They're the ones who show up with the right fluid program for the ground — and can adjust when conditions change at 15 metres.

That's what separates a one-pass bore from a three-day headache.

It's why we built the Blick fluid range around programs, not just products. Match the ground, mix it right, get it done.
What's the toughest ground you've hit this year? Drop it below — we've probably got a program for it.
#HDD #DrillingFluids #WesternAustralia #BlickGroup #UndergroundInfrastructure

BG
Blick Group
Drilling Fluids · Australia & New Zealand · Just now
+ Follow
The cheapest product on your rig is doing the most work.
Soda Ash. Costs almost nothing per bag. But skip it and your bentonite will never hit full yield.

Here's why: hard water — anything above 240 mg/L calcium — stops bentonite from hydrating properly. You end up doubling your gel just to get basic viscosity. That's money down the hole.

The fix takes two minutes. Condition your water with Soda Ash before you add your Max Gel or HDD Gel. Let it work. Then mix your bentonite into water that actually lets it perform.

One $15 step that saves you $200 in wasted product.
Do you test your mix water before adding bentonite? Or just send it?
#DrillingTips #HDD #WaterWell #MaxGel #BlickFluids
👍 Like💬 Comment🔁 Repost📤 Send

Notes from Nexus
Educational angle that positions Blick as the expert advisor — not just selling product but helping crews get more from what they buy. Names Max Gel and HDD Gel. The 240 mg/L calcium figure comes directly from the HDD Gel PDS water quality limits.
BG
Blick Group
Drilling Fluids · Australia & New Zealand · Just now
+ Follow
Cuttings dropping every time the pump stops. That's the sign polymer isn't enough.
Heavy rock. Coarse gravel. The stuff that falls straight back to the bottom the moment you stop to make a connection.

Standard polymer systems can't hold it. They don't gel fast enough.

Carry Max is built for exactly this. It's not a polymer — it's a Mixed Metal Oxide that goes fluid when you're pumping and gels solid the moment flow stops. Cuttings stay suspended. Hole stays clean.

In gravelly ground it also stiffens against the formation face — seals permeable zones and holds the bore open without a filter cake.

Pair it with Max Gel as the base. Get your pH right with Soda Ash first. Let the system yield. Then run the bore.
Running rock or gravel and fighting to keep the hole clean? That's Carry Max territory. Give us a call.
#CarryMax #HDD #DrillingFluids #BlickGroup #RockDrilling

Notes from Nexus
Carry Max is the most differentiated product in the range — nothing else does what MMO does. Avoided technical language (no "thixotropic", no "rheology") but explained the mechanism in plain terms. Named Max Gel and Soda Ash as paired products. No fabricated customer or project — kept it generic.
